message:
data(raptorq): broaden Track-E E5 evidence lineage and add simd-intrinsics to bench command surface

Two related Track-E E5 follow-ups bundled because they touch the same
test surface (raptorq_perf_invariants.rs) and the same canonical
command-bundle string in the source-of-truth profile-pack metadata.

(1) simd-intrinsics in the bench command surface
----------------------------------------------------

Every reproducer command in the Track-E lineage — manifest command
bundles, dual-policy probe repro commands, rollback rehearsal commands,
deterministic replay pre/post commands, e2e validation bundle commands,
and the manual-override command bundle — must now compile the bench
binary with the `simd-intrinsics` feature flag.  Without this flag,
the AVX2/NEON GF(256) kernels are not built into the bench binary,
which means rollback rehearsals and probe captures would silently
exercise scalar paths even when the catalog claims SIMD coverage.

Files updated to add `--features simd-intrinsics`:

- src/raptorq/gf256.rs: GF256_PROFILE_PACK_COMMAND_BUNDLE,
  MANUAL_OVERRIDE_COMMAND_BUNDLE
- benches/raptorq_benchmark.rs: TRACK_E_REPRO_CMD,
  TRACK_E_POLICY_PROBE_REPRO_CMD
- scripts/run_raptorq_e2e.sh: validate_dual_policy_probe_contract
  jq command_bundle / repro_command regex assertions, and the
  bench-smoke-gf256-{primitives,dual-policy} stage commands across
  all profiles
- artifacts/raptorq_optimization_decision_records_v1.json: rollback
  rehearsal commands, deterministic replay pre/post commands, bench
  repro commands, command_surface_split entries (G3-E4 and G3-E5)
- docs/raptorq_baseline_bench_profile.md: dual-policy probe section,
  manual-override placeholder, command-surface-split bullets, and
  the dual-policy probe capture command bundle
- docs/raptorq_optimization_decision_records.md: 2026-02-20 and
  2026-03-05 evidence-alignment update bullets
- tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs:
    e5_live_gf256_catalog_matches_current_x86_default_contract,
    g3_e5_decision_record_command_surface_split_is_explicit,
    track_e_dual_policy_probe_contract_surface_tokens
  assertions all updated to expect the new command surface

(2) E5 closure-readiness dependency lineage broadened
------------------------------------------------------

The E5 closure-readiness dependency in
artifacts/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract_v1.json was
previously named "E5 high-confidence p95/p99 comparator corpus",
which only described one of the four artifacts in the actual
evidence lineage.  The dependency name now reflects the full lineage:
"E5 Track-E evidence lineage (highconf_v1 + v2/v3 history + v4 blocker)".

This makes machine-checkable closure-readiness diagnostics correct:
agents inspecting the dependency table can now see at a glance that
multiple Track-E packets feed E5, not just highconf_v1.

Supporting changes:

- artifacts/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract_v1.json:
    - generated_at_utc bumped to 2026-04-10T02:35:00Z
    - validation.unit_commands gets the new
      g7_expected_loss_contract_docs_are_cross_linked test
    - dependency entry name renamed (above)
- docs/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract.md:
    - Each Track-E artifact now carries an inline role description
      (narrowed closure-status guardrail / historical short-window /
       historical broader interval-proxy / current mixed-signal blocker)
    - New paragraph reaffirming the role of each packet in the lineage
    - Closure-readiness section updated with the new dependency
      shorthand "highconf_v1 + v2/v3 history + v4 blocker" and the
      2026-04-10 refresh date
    - New paragraph naming the current unresolved broader blocker
      packet
- tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs:
    - g7_expected_loss_contract_schema_and_coverage now also asserts
      the renamed E5 dependency entry name and the new docs-cross-link
      unit command
    - g7_expected_loss_contract_docs_are_cross_linked: new lineage
      tokens added (historical short-window directional packet,
      historical broader interval-proxy negative guardrail, current
      mixed-signal raw-sample blocker, lineage shorthand)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+Dependency shorthand: `highconf_v1 + v2/v3 history + v4 blocker`.

 `residual_risk_register`, `go_no_go_decision`) are now attached in

+  - probe-specific `repro_command` stays anchored to `rch exec -- cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- gf256_dual_policy`
 - Captured the support-slice validation bundle (`agent-mail asupersync-36m6p #4585`): `dual_policy_snapshot_exposes_profile_pack_metadata`, `e5_profile_pack_doc_explains_command_bundle_split`, and `e5_profile_pack_doc_mentions_current_x86_default_contract` all pass, and `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets` remains green.

   - Implemented bounded wavefront decode pipeline (`decode_wavefront`) in `src/raptorq/decoder.rs` with fused assembly+peeling in bounded batches and catch-up propagation for deterministic results.

             (.selected_tuning_prefetch_distance | type == "number" and . >= 0 and floor == .) and
             (.selected_tuning_fusion_shape | type == "string" and length > 0) and
             (.rejected_tuning_candidate_ids | type == "string" and length > 0) and
-            (.command_bundle | type == "string" and test("^((rch exec -- )?(env .+ )?cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark -- gf256_primitives)")) and
+            (.command_bundle | type == "string" and test("^((rch exec -- )?(env .+ )?cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- gf256_primitives)")) and
             (.decision_artifact_id | type == "string" and length > 0) and
             (.decision_role | type == "string" and length > 0) and
             (.decision_evidence_status | type == "string" and length > 0) and
@@ -388,7 +388,7 @@ validate_dual_policy_probe_contract() {
             (.tail_confidence_proxy == "criterion_interval_high_endpoint_proxy_p95p99") and
             (.replay_pointer | type == "string" and length > 0) and
             (.artifact_path | type == "string" and length > 0) and
-            (.repro_command | type == "string" and test("^((rch exec -- )?cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark -- gf256_dual_policy)")) and
+            (.repro_command | type == "string" and test("^((rch exec -- )?cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- gf256_dual_policy)")) and
             (if .selected_tuning_candidate_id == "manual-env-override-unbacked"
                 then
                     .tuning_corpus_id == "manual-env-override-unbacked" and
@@ -397,7 +397,7 @@ validate_dual_policy_probe_contract() {
                     .selected_tuning_prefetch_distance == 0 and
                     .selected_tuning_fusion_shape == "unknown" and
                     .rejected_tuning_candidate_ids == "none" and
-                    .command_bundle == "rch exec -- env <captured ASUPERSYNC_GF256_* override fields> cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark -- gf256_primitives" and
+                    .command_bundle == "rch exec -- env <captured ASUPERSYNC_GF256_* override fields> cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- gf256_primitives" and
                     .decision_artifact_id == "manual_env_override_unbacked" and
                     .decision_role == "runtime_override_not_canonical_profile_selection" and
                     .decision_evidence_status == "runtime-override-unbacked" and
@@ -422,7 +422,7 @@ validate_dual_policy_probe_contract() {
                     .selected_tuning_unroll >= 1 and
                     .selected_tuning_fusion_shape != "unknown" and
                     .rejected_tuning_candidate_ids != "none" and
-                    .command_bundle == "rch exec -- cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark -- gf256_primitives" and
+                    .command_bundle == "rch exec -- cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- gf256_primitives" and
